# LLM Observability
## Purpose
Make LLM systems measurable in production through tracing, prompt/version tracking, cost analysis, and evaluation loops.

## Inputs Required
- LLM features and model providers in scope.
- Prompt lifecycle and versioning approach.
- Cost, latency, and quality objectives.
- Privacy constraints for stored prompts and outputs.

## Step-by-Step Workflow
1. Enable request and response tracing for LLM calls.
2. Tag traces with feature, model, user, and version metadata.
3. Capture token usage, latency, and cost dimensions.
4. Add quality evaluation signals (manual or automated).
5. Build dashboards and alerts for regressions and spikes.
6. Document incident triage and rollback process for prompts/models.

## Validation Checks
- Every production LLM path emits trace metadata.
- Cost and latency can be queried per feature and model.
- Prompt versions are attributable to output behavior changes.
- Alerts exist for abnormal spend, error rate, or latency.

## Output Format
- Observability architecture summary.
- Trace taxonomy and metadata contract.
- Monitoring and evaluation checklist.

## Integration Hooks (which agents use this)
- `observability-specialist`
- `lead-ai-ethics-officer`

## Anti-Patterns
- Shipping LLM features with no tracing or cost visibility.
- Mixing prompt versions without trace metadata.
- Ignoring regression signals after model/provider changes.

## External References (official docs URL from tech stack)
- https://docs.helicone.ai/
- https://langfuse.com/docs
